channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran slams the GCC and the US for ‘interventionist’ statement: What we know
item.description: Tehran rejects US-GCC pressure over missiles, the Strait of Hormuz and regional armed groups.

item.title: South Korean court jails former first lady for seven years in bribery case
item.description: Kim Keon Hee was sentenced for taking bribes including luxury items in return for political favours.

item.title: Iraq’s new PM launches anticorruption campaign, but is it enough?
item.description: Al-Zaidi campaign to combat corruption seizes $86m, properties, vehicles and gold. But analysts say he needs to do more.

item.title: New York’s primaries signal a new force in US politics
item.description: Progressive, Muslim and Arab American candidates are challenging old limits on debate over Israel and Palestine.

item.title: Aid groups urge swift action to avert ‘atrocities’ in Sudan’s el-Obeid
item.description: The city is at risk of an 'imminent ground offensive' by the RSF paramilitary and its allies, 38 NGOs warn.

item.title: Hezbollah demands Israel leave Lebanon ‘unconditionally’ as US talks extend
item.description: As Lebanon-Israel strive for agreement, Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem accepts 'no normalisation' and 'no gains for Israel'

item.title: Thermal cameras reveal intense heat for outdoor workers in Europe
item.description: Thermal cameras reveal intense heat for outdoor workers in Europe

item.title: Everything we know so far about the deadly earthquakes in Venezuela
item.description: Everything we know so far about the deadly earthquakes in Venezuela

item.title: Which countries have pledged aid to Venezuela after powerful earthquakes?
item.description: Countries in the Americas and beyond are sending aid, medical supplies and paramedics to Venezuela.

item.title: Ukraine decimates Russian logistics, bringing chaos to Crimea
item.description: Kyiv circumvents air defences to target oil supplies, power stations, convoys and bridges, starving Russian front line.

item.title: The Pakistan artisans still hand-stitching the world’s footballs
item.description: For generations, Sialkot made footballs for the world. Now the hands behind the game face an uncertain future.

item.title: New synthetic drugs, cocaine and meth booming, warns UN
item.description: A spike in potent synthetic drugs comes as manufacturers respond to geopolitical changes and look to increase profits.

item.title: Climate change the culprit for Europe’s ‘most severe’ heatwave: Report
item.description: The extreme June temperatures would have been 'virtually impossible' 50 years ago, says World Weather Attribution group.

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: What happens in the first 72 hours?
item.description: Experts say the first three days following a serious quake often determine how many lives can still be saved.

item.title: Egypt vs Iran at World Cup 2026 – Salah, prediction, lineups, how to watch
item.description: Mohamed Salah's Egypt and Mehdi Taremi's Iran are both in the hunt for the FIFA World Cup knockouts in Group G.

item.title: Can Europe’s historic cities survive a warming climate?
item.description: Heatwaves are forcing the continent to choose between preserving the past and adapting for the future.

item.title: IAEA demands verification of Iran nuclear ambitions amid ‘statement war’
item.description: Watchdog's call for 'strong system' comes as US and Iran issue conflicting claims over ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: Who has profited most from the war on Iran?
item.description: Defence contractors, energy companies and investment banks saw profits soar as war and uncertainty upended markets.

item.title: Baby pulled alive from rubble after Venezuela earthquake
item.description: Baby pulled alive from rubble after Venezuela earthquake

item.title: Russia reports downing 660 Ukrainian drones, denies seeking Belarus war aid
item.description: The drone attack is one of Kyiv's biggest of the war, sustaining pressure on Russia as it struggles with fuel shortages.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Friday schedule, predictions, standings, knockout race
item.description: World Cup knockout places are on the line as Groups G, H and I conclude, with fixtures, predictions, and standings.

item.title: Mother gives birth amid Venezuela earthquake devastation
item.description: Mother gives birth amid Venezuela earthquake devastation

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Why is Caracas so vulnerable?
item.description: Inadequate infrastructure in Caracas due to underfunding intensified destruction from the tremors.

item.title: ‘London cooking’: Why can’t the UK cope with the heat; when will it adapt?
item.description: A nation used to cold, rainy weather is starting to discuss the value of overhauling its systems.

item.title: ‘Affordability crisis’: How the Western housing crisis spiralled
item.description: Rising rents and prices outpacing wages provoke global debate: Is housing a basic right or an investment asset?
